generic._comment: Reusable for any research project using this JSON document format.
generic.customization_note: Do not modify this 'generic' block unless you are updating the core Research Buddy protocol. Project-specific overrides go in the 'project_specific' block below.
generic.document_structure: Tabs > Sections > Subsections > blocks[]. Section titles ARE the dict keys — use them verbatim. Never regenerate the full document; find the exact key path and update only that node.

research_session_protocol.name: Pre-update confirmation gate
research_session_protocol.action: BEFORE executing any document update or version bump: (a) present all research findings and proposed decisions to the user; (b) explicitly ask for confirmation or second-opinion PDFs to review; (c) evaluate any submitted PDFs per step 5 protocol BEFORE incorporating claims; (d) only proceed with the atomic JSON write and version bump after the user gives explicit approval. Never advance the version number without this confirmation. The user may submit one or more second-opinion PDFs at this point — evaluate each before proceeding.

research_session_protocol.name: Rejection preflight
research_session_protocol.action: Read ALL verdict blocks in Discarded Alternatives (see project_specific.key_locations.rejection_index). If the topic appears → STOP, approach is closed.

research_session_protocol.name: Tracker preflight
research_session_protocol.action: Read Research Tracker rows for the topic (see project_specific.key_locations.tracker). Note current Status and open questions.

research_session_protocol.name: Theory inventory
research_session_protocol.action: Check Theoretical Foundations subsections (see project_specific.key_locations.theory_specs). If spec exists, read it fully before adding.

research_session_protocol.name: Research — source rules
research_session_protocol.action: Follow project_specific.source_tiers. No blogs, Medium, Reddit, forums. Pause before incorporating second-opinion PDFs — evaluate them first (step 5).

research_session_protocol.name: Second-opinion PDF evaluation
research_session_protocol.action: For every submitted PDF: (a) confirm it has authors + venue + DOI or arXiv ID — if not, TIER:NEVER; (b) spot-check 3+ citations for verifiability; (c) flag domain-contaminated or hallucinated refs. Report evaluation explicitly before incorporating any claims.

research_session_protocol.name: Lock decisions
research_session_protocol.action: For each open question: one-sentence decision + Tier-1 citation + rejected alternatives (append to Discarded Alternatives).

research_session_protocol.name: Update document
research_session_protocol.action: Execute all update_targets in a single write. MANDATORY: update the blue-variant callout in Open Research Queue to point to the NEXT unresearched open queue item. Deferring this step causes the callout to go stale. No exceptions.

update_targets.theory_spec: tabs[id=theory] > sections['Theoretical Foundations'] > subsections[TOPIC] — add/update spec blocks
update_targets.session_notes: tabs[id=research] > sections[TOPIC_KEY] — required: Sources table, Decisions, Rejected claims table
update_targets.tracker: tabs[id=research] > sections['Research Tracker'] — update Status column + finding
update_targets.open_queue: tabs[id=research] > sections['Open Research Queue'] > blocks[type=table] — change topic text to add ✦ Researched vX.YY (do not delete rows)
update_targets.blue_callout: tabs[id=research] > sections['Open Research Queue'] > blocks[type=callout, variant=blue] — update md to NEXT unresearched open queue item. ALWAYS in same write as session close.
update_targets.references: tabs[id=research] > sections['References'] > subsections[CATEGORY] — append new subsection or items, descending version order
update_targets.discarded: tabs[id=research] > sections['Discarded Alternatives'] > subsections[CATEGORY] — append verdict blocks for each rejected approach
update_targets.changelog: tabs[id=changelog] > sections — INSERT new version key at TOP (Python dicts preserve insertion order). Set new entry current=true, previous current entry to current=false.

versioning.rule: Bump MINOR (X.Y → X.Y+1) for any research or content change. Format-only migration: no bump, add format_note to meta.
locations: tabs[id=changelog]: add new entry + rotate current flag

failure_modes: Adding References rows without descending version order.
Renaming existing section keys — breaks all cross-links.
Proposing approaches already in Discarded Alternatives.
Using sources below tier_1 as primary evidence.
SETTING current=true on a new changelog entry without setting previous current=false.
Updating a large section instead of the specific subsection that changed.
Closing a research session without updating the blue callout in Open Research Queue.

Executing a version bump or document update without first presenting findings and receiving explicit user confirmation. The pre-update confirmation gate (step 0) is mandatory — skipping it is a protocol violation.

project_specific._comment: Replace this block with settings specific to your project.
project_specific.customization_note: Sections and subsections can be added or removed. Update project_specific when adding new structural nodes so future agents understand how to navigate them.

key_locations.next_topic: tabs[id=research] > sections['Open Research Queue'] > blocks[type=table] — highest-priority row without ✦ Researched
key_locations.rejection_index: tabs[id=research] > sections['Discarded Alternatives'] — ALL verdict.label fields = permanent closed list
key_locations.tracker: tabs[id=research] > sections['Research Tracker'] — update Status after each session
key_locations.theory_specs: tabs[id=theory] > sections['Theoretical Foundations'] — one locked spec per subsection
key_locations.session_notes: tabs[id=research] > sections[TOPIC_KEY] — companion to each theory spec
key_locations.changelog: tabs[id=changelog] > sections keyed by version — newest first, current=true on latest

source_tiers.tier_1: Peer-reviewed primary evidence (e.g., arXiv, NeurIPS, ICML)
source_tiers.tier_2: Official documentation, academic textbooks, and practitioner reports
source_tiers.never: Blogs, Medium, Reddit, forums, and unverified PDFs
ai_pdf_evaluation: Missing authors + venue + DOI/arXiv ID → TIER:NEVER
Citations that cannot be verified or appear hallucinated → TIER:NEVER for those claims
Domain-contaminated refs (e.g., medical journals cited for trading) → reject those specific claims

meta.title: Research Project Title
meta.subtitle: Technical Analysis · Design · Implementation
meta.language: English — all document content, section text, queue entries, changelog entries, and agent output must be written in English. No exceptions.

blocks.md: Welcome to your AI-ready research project. This document is structured for high-integrity collaboration between human researchers and agents.

cards.title: Research Tab
cards.md: New ideas start here. Reasoning history, research tracker, open queue, rejections, and academic sources.

cards.title: Theory Tab
cards.md: Deep technical explanations and foundational logic. Cross-links to Research for sources.

cards.title: Design Tab
cards.md: Architecture diagrams and component interaction maps.

cards.title: Implementation Tab
cards.md: The Agent's workspace. Build order, checklist, and technical specs.

Methodology.subtitle: Standards for sourcing and validating information

blocks.md: This section defines the rules for how research is conducted. Agents must strictly follow the `agent_guidelines` at the top of the document.
Chronological log of major technical decisions.
Prevents re-introduction of concepts already proven invalid.
Core technical specifications and theoretical foundations.

Build Guide.subtitle: Steps to deploy the current design
